insight: Thorium Dynamo reduces strategic variety by forcing a near-universal opener (DD/D1 ~95% of the time for P1) rather than creating genuine decision points, making it a homogenizing force despite enabling certain BP strategies.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion about whether player balance or power balance matters more, with masn6811 arguing Thorium's forced opener is a bigger problem than small P1/P2 winrate skews.

insight: Centurion is a major homogenizing force in Prismata because having it in the set causes both players to buy roughly the same tech structure every game (Conduit + 2 Blastforges + Animus), eliminating strategic variety in openings.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 identifying units that reduce game variety by forcing specific tech purchases, contrasting with units that create genuine decisions.

insight: Grimbotch has a heavily P1-favored winrate (~34% spread for P2 in masn6811's sample), while Urban Sentry sits at ~55% P1 winrate, making it one of the more player-imbalanced units. By contrast, Odin is only 52% P1 globally.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 discussing player balance statistics citing prismata-stats data and personal game records to illustrate which units skew P1/P2 winrates.

insight: Chrono Filter combined with Wincer (chrono wincer) heavily favors P2, yet this pair received less balance attention than single units like Trinity Drone, since two-unit interactions are inherently less common and harder to address systematically.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 critiquing the prioritization of single-unit player balance over two-unit interaction balance in the development team's approach.

insight: In sets with both Amporilla and Centurion, Centurion is situationally skippable — a player can rush Amporilla without buying Centurion and win, but the interaction is nuanced rather than a blanket rule.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion about whether Amporilla warps games as much as legendaries and whether players should always buy both Ampo and Centurion when both are available.

insight: Amporilla's supply of 4 is meaningful because it ensures Ampo remains viable even when Apollo is present in the set, though supplies 2–4 provide value without imposing the negative gameplay experience that the first copy can create.
author: mrguy888
context: Debate on whether Amporilla should be legendary, with mrguy888 arguing supplies beyond 1 add value without gameplay cost and shadourow noting Apollo interaction.

insight: Sacrificing a unit with counters on it is not possible under Prismata rules, which constrains any custom unit designs that require sacrificing a Drone as an additional cost, since Drones may have build counters.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 reviewing a fan-designed unit that required sacrificing a Drone as an additional purchase cost and explaining why the rules prevent this.

insight: The Frontline + Fragile combination does not function correctly in Prismata's engine due to spaghetti code in the frontline implementation written by a developer who no longer works at Lunarch, making it effectively impossible to ship without a full recode.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion about whether any unit has or could have both Frontline and Fragile, with mrguy888 explaining the technical constraint.

insight: In Prismata community terminology, 'shock' refers specifically to a unit that provides more threat per turn than the number of tech pieces in its cost (e.g., Drake costs 2 Blue = 2 techs but deals 4 threat, so it is shock). It does not simply mean 'temporary damage source'.
author: mrguy888
context: mrguy888 correcting shadourow's definition of 'shock' during a discussion about what constitutes consistent shock units, with shadourow initially defining it as any temporary damage source.

insight: Hannibull serves as an effective counter to Wincer because it can threaten lethal damage (ideally 7 damage) before Wincer's bulls come online, with the optimal timing being the earliest moment where the attack total does not lose to Hannibull itself.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 explaining anti-Wincer timing strategy in the context of discussing a custom Isofield unit as a potential Wincer counter.

insight: Click abilities that trigger off buying Walls (or other units purchased the same turn) are illegal in Prismata's current rules — all resonator-style effects (Savior, Resophore, Amporilla) use start-of-turn triggers specifically to avoid this ambiguity.
author: mrguy888
context: mrguy888 ruling a fan-designed Wall-resonator unit illegal because its click ability could count Walls purchased on the same turn it was activated.

insight: When a unit sacrifices itself to construct a new unit (as Gaussite Symbiote does to make Goose Charges, or Infusion to make Husks), this is the correct mechanical way to implement a 'permanent loss of Blocker' effect without needing a new 'lose ability' mechanic. Creating a new token unit is preferable to introducing a novel mechanic that does nothing new.
author: mrguy888
context: mrguy888 advising on how to implement a custom Armored Angel unit that loses its Blocker keyword after being clicked, explaining the design principle of preferring new cards over new mechanics.
